reconstructed_sketch: From the chapter context alone (2.46-2.48 already shipped, 2.50-2.53 follow), an engineering analog at position 2.49 might address the practitioner's relationship to fruit-driven action vs detached action. But the specific contributions of 2.49 — the explicit COMPARATIVE RANKING ('far below'), the kṛpaṇa-tagging of the fruit-attached actor as PITIABLE (not failing, not misguided — pitiable), and the refuge-in-buddhi grammar (śaraṇam) — would not be reconstructible. From the chapter alone an analog might say 'detached action is better than attached action'; only with 2.49's specific Sanskrit (dūreṇa avaram, kṛpaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ, buddhau śaraṇam anviccha) would the analog know to (a) make a comparative ranking, (b) call the fruit-attached actor pitiable, and (c) frame buddhi as a refuge. The Engineer-A-vs-Engineer-B contrast is 2.49-specific; the chapter context alone gives only the general theme.
verdict_reason: The verse adds strong specific signal not reconstructible from chapter alone.
